title: Sea Otter Recovery Program
description: Recovering sea otter population from near-extinction to ecosystem engineer. Otters eat sea urchins, which allows kelp forests to thrive.
impact: Each otter manages ~20 lbs of sea urchins daily = system healing

title: Kelp Forest Restoration
description: Restoring giant kelp forests that were decimated by sea urchin populations. Kelp sequester carbon, provide habitat, and support food webs.
learningTitle: OTEC: Ocean Thermal Energy as Healing
impact: 1 acre of kelp = 10x carbon sequestration of terrestrial forest

title: Sustainable Fishing Partnership
description: Working with local fisheries to implement sustainable practices that protect marine ecosystems while supporting livelihoods.
impact: Fair burden & benefit distribution in ocean conservation

title: Biodiversity Monitoring Network
description: Real-time monitoring of ocean biodiversity using acoustic sensors, underwater cameras, and citizen science reports from divers.
impact: Early detection of ecosystem changes enables rapid response

title: Ocean Acidification Research
description: Measuring pH, carbonate saturation, and species responses to ocean acidification. Connecting local observations to global climate patterns.
learningTitle: Tikkun Olam: Repair of the World
impact: Understanding root causes enables systemic solutions

title: Visitor Education & Engagement
description: Converting 500K annual visitors into ocean advocates through interactive experiences, decision trees, citizen science participation, and sustained community involvement.
impact: Transform visitors from observers to active ocean stewards
